---
title: Project Proposal
author:
- Collin Farrell
- Paarth Mahajan
- Aayush Sharma
- Tony Zheng
format:
    html:
        embed-resources: true
---

## Project Title

## Research Questions
### (RQ.1)
Does the temperature of a day affect the # of rentals after accounting for other weather conditions (e.g. precipitation, humidity, and wind speed) and user type?

### (RQ.2)
Do weather conditions affect registered vs. casual users differently?

## Model

The response variable of our model will be the number of rentals each hour (both for casual users, and registered users). Denoting the number of bike rentals at hour $i$ to be $Y_i$ (one of `casual` or `registered` in the dataset), we assume
$$
Y_i \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda_i) \\
$$

with link function
$$
\log(\lambda_i) = X^T\beta
$$

for $X \in \mathbb{R}^{(p+1) \times n}$ and $\beta \in \mathbb{R}^{p + 1}$, where $n$ is the number of samples in the hour dataset times two (with samples of registered and casual rentals at each hour), and $p$ is the number of predictors we're including, or the number of rows in the [Predictors](#predictors) section. Then, we would have an intercept term $\beta_0$ with $X_{i, 1} = 1$.

## Predictors

Our model will include:

| Predictor | Reason |
| -- | ----------- |
| `temp`, `windspeed`, `humidity` | To control for the effect of the different aspects of the weather on the number of bike rentals |
| `weathersit` | To examine the effect of the amount of precipitation on the number of bike rentals, after accounting for other parts of the weather |
| `userType` | The user type for the count (whether the count was of registered or casual users). Included to account for the effect of being a registered user by itself on rentals |
| Interaction between `userType` and all other predictors | To see how registered users are affected by each weather effect compared to casual users |
